message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): DistributedHttpClient is Send + Sync (tick 364)

Added `distributed_http_client_is_send_and_sync` — a compile-time
type-level assertion that `DistributedHttpClient` implements
`Send + Sync + 'static`.

Why this matters: for the distributed RPC use case we want to store
the client in `Arc<DistributedHttpClient>` and share it across
concurrent request tasks. That requires Send + Sync + 'static. If a
future refactor of the inner asupersync HttpClient (or any wrapping
future) adds a non-Send or non-Sync field, this test fails at build
time — before any callsite would hit the restriction.

Uses the standard zero-runtime-cost pattern:
  fn assert_send<T: Send>() {}
  fn assert_sync<T: Sync>() {}
  fn assert_send_sync_static<T: Send + Sync + 'static>() {}
  assert_send::<DistributedHttpClient>();
  ...

The `#[test]` body only contains the assertion-function calls; no
runtime exercise. The test passes iff the trait bounds hold at
compile time.

Complements `distributed_http_client_concurrent_gets` (tick 318)
which exercises concurrent *creation* of per-task clients — this
tick proves concurrent *sharing* via Arc is type-safe.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 09s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
